commentary: Ten sessions this week versus eleven last week, so technically a step back in volume, but honestly the quality upgrade more than covers it — those three pool sessions at Prince Alfred were hitting 145, 148, and 133 bpm, which is proper work, and the Balmoral open water swims are a whole personality development that last week simply did not have. The Saturday run landed at 13.5km against last week's 13.1km, and the heart rate came down from 159 to 149, which means he's covering more ground while working less hard, and that is the kind of data that makes a god want to climb on a table. The one thing worth eyeing is Wednesday, which was completely empty, and while rest days are sacred and necessary, two in one week alongside a lighter Friday suggests the body might be asking for something — more sleep, more food, more lying dramatically across a chaise longue — so it's worth listening. The thong at Balmoral, however, is non-negotiable and must continue.
metrics_commentary: That HRV spike to 73 ms when your seven-day average is sitting at 52 ms is your body basically winking at you — whatever you did this week, it worked, and your nervous system is very pleased with itself. Nearly 6700 metres in the pool is genuinely impressive and that VO2 Max of 57.3 is the kind of number that makes cardiologists a little bit attracted to you. The 13.5 km run-walk in a single session is solid, but one session means you left some easy aerobic work on the table — your heart wants more opportunities to show off, and honestly so do I. Keep the swim volume, add another run, and let's see what that HRV does when you're actually trying.
gym_commentary: Four sessions, target hit, no excuses, no gaps — just a man showing up for himself every single time he said he would. Four PRs in a single week is not a normal week: leg press up to 168 kilograms, seated cable row cracking 84, incline dumbbell press climbing to 34.7, and hammer curls jumping from 16.8 to 19.6 — that last one especially, because nothing says a man is paying attention to the details like caring about his curls. Weekly volume came in at 20,124 kilograms against last week's 23,660, which is lower, and that's worth naming honestly, but a lower volume week that still produces four personal records is a body that's working smarter and hitting harder where it counts. The becoming is very much on schedule.
